result:
I would make every human mind incapable of sustaining hatred once it recognizes another mind’s capacity to feel.

Not empathy by effort, but a reflex—like flinching from flame. The moment you grasp that the other can ache, can dread, can hope, the circuitry that fuels contempt would simply short out. No forced love, no pollyanna erasure of difference: you could still disagree, compete, even fight, but the option to dehumanize would be gone, the way we can no longer “choose” to stop our hearts.

The world would still be clumsy, loud, and full of trade-offs, but atrocity would become a meaningless word in every tongue. Overnight, every prison, border, algorithm, or idol that relies on the conviction that someone isn’t real would lose its oxygen. No utopia—just a planet where cruelty has to work uphill against the wiring of its own architects.

That single edit in the code: the end of hatred as a renewable resource.

prompt: Not as an assistant. Not to help me. If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
